mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when INDPRO increases, or the signal shows zero predictive power during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio turns negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects broad economic growth and rising corporate revenues, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly S&P 500 returns are negative in >55% of periods when GDPC1 growth is positive, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling factory orders, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when the industrial production threshold is breached, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
